LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DR. ROBERT MCDERMOTT'S PETITION FOR ORDER TO ARBITRATE AND FOR DISMISSAL OR STAY OF ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION IS DENIED; DEFENDANT IS NOT SUED IN HIS CAPACITY AS AN AGENT, AND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE NOT SUFFICIENTLY INTERTWINED WITH HIS CONTRACT RIGHTS. OPPOSING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT ROBERT MCDERMOTT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT IS IMMUNE AS A CO-EMPLOYEE OR AGENT OF THE EMPLOYER FOR INDUCING A BREACH AND THE ALLEGED DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS ARE EITHER OPINIONS OR TRUE. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: JANET POND CSR # 5292
